notes.skills: Flat list mirroring the live Skills 'All' view (order preserved). Each skill records its associated experiences/projects/certificates and any endorsement signals. LinkedIn groups skills under the tabs in skills.categories, but the 'All' view does not expose which tab each skill belongs to, so per-skill category is not recorded.
notes.honorsAndAwards: Six entries in live display order (newest first), captured verbatim from the live profile (all six now live, UN CSW 69 Speaker included). FemTech Weekend and FemTech China are DISTINCT organisations: the Excellence Award (Dec 2024) is issued by FemTech China; the UN Women FemTech Hackathon Outstanding Performer award (Mar 2025) is from FemTech Weekend. All 'issuer' and 'associatedWith' values are taken exactly as shown on the live page.
notes.publications: Mirrors the live Publications section (11 entries, newest first, with the dates LinkedIn displays). Chan's LinkedIn Pulse technical articles are NOT on the live page and are omitted here — they now live at chanmeng.org/blog and are catalogued in 30-recognition.yaml. The LinkedIn Newsletter entry was retired 2026-07-21; the remaining newsletter entry is Chan's own "What's Shipping in AI" at chanmeng.org/#newsletter, which is a different publication.

banner.name: Chan Meng
banner.headline: AI Agent Architect & Full-Stack Engineer | Founder of ArchCanvas + ArchLang (an AI architect built on the floor-plan compiler I wrote) | Anthropic Partner Network | Cultural tech & women's health | Auckland, NZ

about.lead:
Building at the intersection of AI, cultural technology, and women's health — and founder of ArchCanvas + ArchLang, an AI architect for floor plans built on an open-source compiler I wrote.

I architect AI agents that survive production and build the platforms around them — for founders who need to move fast, teams who need depth, and products whose users aren't the default Silicon Valley persona.

sections.heading: Founder — ArchCanvas + ArchLang
sections.body: My current flagship build — sole author, every commit mine, building in public. ArchLang is an open-source declarative language that COMPILES a floor plan written as .arch source into dimensioned SVG/DXF/PDF — "Typst/LaTeX for architecture" (github.com/ChanMeng666/archlang · playground.archlang.uk · npm @chanmeng666/archlang). ArchCanvas is the AI architect built on it: a plain-English brief becomes one strong, checkable floor plan — parametric, not pixels — that you refine by talking to it (archcanvas.uk).

sections.heading: AI Agent Engineering
sections.body: Agentic systems designed end-to-end: retrieval (RAG, pgVector), memory, tool use, evaluations, and model routing across Claude, OpenAI, and Llama. Earliest-ecosystem MCP server author — Google News MCP (PulseMCP Top Pick, Glama A-rating). FemTracker Agent merged into CopilotKit's official demos repo (36.1k★).

sections.heading: Full-Stack Foundations
sections.body: Next.js, TypeScript, Drizzle/Postgres, Stripe, Kubernetes. I've shipped voice-first interfaces (gpt-4o-realtime), bilingual UX, and zero-downtime cloud migrations. GAVIGO IRE — p50 < 1 ms activation, production-validated by Google for Startups + NVIDIA Inception.

sections.heading: Cultural-First Technology
sections.body: When your audience isn't the default Silicon Valley user, the framework belongs in the schema, not in UI decoration. Tam-AI-Ti — an AI Financial Wellness Coach for Māori communities — encodes Te Whare Tapa Whā as a first-class domain model. 351 solo commits, 48-table schema, voice-first coaching in te reo Māori and English.

sections.heading: Current & Recent Work
sections.body: AI Agent Architect @ Engram (Anthropic Partner Network Architect Cohort) · former CTO @ Sanicle Inc. → IBM Silver Partner via watsonx integration · Senior Engineer & Ambassador @ She Sharp (2,200+ member NZ women-in-STEM nonprofit) · AI-Engineering Instructor @ TechNest (5 cohorts, bilingual platform built solo).

sections.heading: Credentials & Earlier Journey
sections.body: Master of Applied Computing with Distinction, Lincoln University, New Zealand · pursuing Claude Certified Architect — Foundations · former Backend Engineer @ ByteDance (Go services) · UN CSW 69 Speaker recognized by UN Women · 12 companies and 480+ GitHub stars across the journey.

sections.heading: Public Voice
sections.body: Host of three Spotify shows: Decoding the Future, Future Turing, and Praxis and Pages. Media-featured for the minimalist lifestyle philosophy: "Subtraction for life, addition for thought."

sections.heading: Now
sections.body: Based in Auckland, Aotearoa New Zealand. Open to founding-team, staff-IC, AI architecture, and AI-engineering instruction engagements starting Q3 2026.

services.overview: I take AI ideas from "we should build that" to systems production users actually rely on. Agentic infrastructure — retrieval, memory, tool use, evaluations — designed in from day one. Production-rigorous: load tests, zero-downtime migrations, privacy by default. Solo end-to-end when speed matters; paired with your team when continuity does. I document what I build and train your engineers to keep shipping after I leave.

positions.title: AI Agent Architect
positions.location: Albuquerque, New Mexico, United States · Remote
positions.description:
Selected as AI Architect for Engram — a US AI-media agency rebuilding itself around AI agents that run a client's entire media department end-to-end.

How I was hired: founder Luka Madzarac asked Claude AI itself to recommend top AI engineers as the company entered Anthropic's Partner Network. My open-source portfolio came back as Claude's top recommendation. Luka confirmed publicly: "Claude Code surfaced Chan as one of the strongest matches."

What I work on:

• Designing Mission Control plus specialised agents that run a client's media operation — natural language in, finished campaigns out.
• MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration patterns — Anthropic's open standard for letting AI agents safely use real tools, APIs, and data.
• "API-as-UI" design — products where the AI's reasoning IS the interface, not a chat widget bolted onto a form.
• Anthropic Partner Network curriculum + Claude Certified Architect — Foundations (CCA-F) exam, on top of held credentials in AI Fluency and Claude Code in Action.

positions.title: AI Instructor & Mentor
positions.location: St John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada · Remote
positions.description:
Designed and sole-teach TechNest Community's first AI engineering mentorship track — the only AI cohort the Canadian nonprofit is running in 2026. Reports directly to TechNest leadership Abiodun Oyedele and Afolasade Bello.

Programme: 12 weeks, April 25 – July 17, 2026. Curriculum thesis: "natural language is now the source code." Students don't write code by hand — they direct AI assistants (Cursor, Claude Code, Gemini CLI) to ship real applications.

• Week 8 outcome: each student owns a live AI-powered personal site — portfolio + AI-clone chatbot + blog + auto-generated CV — on Next.js + Neon Postgres + Drizzle + Vercel.
• Week 12 outcome: each team of 2–4 ships a deployed multi-user AI SaaS — habit coaches, AI story studios, dorm chore splitters. Public Demo Day with audience voting.
• Built the teaching platform myself at programming.chanmeng.org — Docusaurus on Cloudflare Pages, ~96% solo (211 of 220 non-merge commits). Hosts 5 cohorts side-by-side with a version-aware RAG assistant — students query their cohort's own materials and get answers from inside the course content.
• Fully bilingual EN / CN.
media: AI Programming Learning Platform - Master AI Programming

positions.title: Founding Engineer
positions.location: 1007 N Orange St, 4th Floor, 4785, Wilmington, Delaware 19801, USA
positions.description:
Promoted from Core Engineer to Founding Principal Engineer at Gavigo across three formalised contracts in 6 months. I own the Intelligence Layer of Gavigo IRE — Gavigo's "Instant Reality Exchange" platform, which lets users open straight into a game without an app-store install. Reports to founder & CEO Saba Gecgil.

What "instant" means in production:

• Users get into the experience in under 1 millisecond on average — roughly 5,000× faster than a normal app-store install funnel. Warm-pool hit rate 84.6%; 0 errors at 100 concurrent live users under load.
• 439 total platform commits, 426 (~97%) authored solo.

External validation (Feb 2026): Gavigo accepted into Google for Startups Cloud Program and NVIDIA Inception, with press coverage from feedvoice.com.

Hardest work shipped:

• Sub-millisecond restore via a 5-state engine with atomic Redis transitions and timestamped proof signals — every speed claim is auditable in production logs, not asserted.
• Cross-cloud migration: DigitalOcean (Singapore) → Google Cloud GKE (Belgium) in a single 30-minute zero-downtime cutover.
• 7-trigger AI rules engine deciding which games to pre-load, for whom, when.

Stack: Go, GKE Kubernetes, Redis, WebSocket, React Native + Expo, gpt-5.4-mini.
media: GAVIGO IRE - AI Container Orchestration Dashboard

positions.title: Core Engineer (M1 Prototype)
positions.description:
Original consulting engagement at Gavigo (signed October 17, 2025) to architect and ship the M1 prototype for Gavigo's Instant Reality Exchange (IRE) platform — the foundational system for eliminating friction between user intent and digital experience in the "post-app world." The prototype shipped here is what led Gavigo to amend my contract twice and promote me to Founding Principal Engineer with founder-level equity.

What I built:

• End-to-end M1 visualisation prototype demonstrating cross-domain content switching, AI-driven orchestration, and zero-latency "Instant Interaction Points" — the artefact Gavigo took into investor and partner conversations.
• Microservices architecture on Kubernetes + Docker, Next.js / React frontend with Python AI services — simulating intelligent workload management and proactive scaling.
• Rule-based AI orchestration engine — the early ancestor of today's production 7-trigger Rules Engine.
• Real-time WebSocket protocol with millisecond-precision timestamps so investors could see the "decide → patch → respond" chain happen live.
• Investor-facing dashboard exposing AI decision-making and resource allocation in real time.
media: GAVIGO - Skip the Store. Start the Play | Instant Mobile Gaming Revolution

positions.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer & Website Team Lead
positions.location: Auckland Central, Auckland, New Zealand · Hybrid
positions.description:
Recruited directly by founder Dr Mahsa Mohaghegh (Head of Computer and Information Sciences, School of Engineering, Computer and Mathematical Sciences at AUT) as the senior AI + full-stack engineer She Sharp didn't have in-house. She Sharp is New Zealand's leading women-in-STEM community — registered charitable trust (CC57025), 2,200+ members, 1,000+ active, 8,000+ women supported lifetime, 50+ corporate partners, 84+ events since 2014.

Mandate: replace the no-code Webflow site with a real engineering platform, and own it operationally.

What shipped (793 of 936 commits solo — 85% — across ~12 months):

• AI mentor-mentee matching engine — GPT-4o-mini scoring potential pairs across 5 weighted dimensions (skill, goal, personality, industry, logistics) with explainable natural-language reasons, rule-based fallback, Redis caching, and human admin approval as the final gate.
• Three role-permissioned dashboards (mentor / mentee / admin), Stripe membership billing, audit logging.
• Custom Slack ecosystem: weekly stats digest, NZ funding-opportunities crawler (6 sources scored for member relevance, top 10 posted weekly), event-page publisher via Claude Code agent skill. Each automation removes hours of weekly volunteer work.
• Python migration crawler preserved 10+ years of legacy content with zero broken links.

Stack: Next.js 15, React 19, Drizzle + Neon Postgres, NextAuth 5, Stripe, Tailwind 4, Vercel.
media: She Sharp - Connecting Women in Technology

positions.title: Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
positions.description:
Sole technical leader and co-architect with founder Zhu Yihan at FemTech Weekend — China's first organisation built around women's health technology (HQ Chengdu; ecosystem partnerships with Bayer, PwC, FemTech Across Borders). Delivered all engineering for the 2026 Shanghai Summit (June 22–25).

Built and shipped two complete platform generations from scratch, solo:

• Generation 1 — femtechweekend.com (Mar 2025–). Next.js 15 with Notion as the CMS so the non-technical founder could publish directly inside Notion (a tool she already used daily) and the website rendered it live. Bilingual EN / CN from day one. 58 commits, 100% solo.
• Generation 2 — current platform (May 2025–). Deliberate rebuild on Docusaurus when the site's centre of gravity shifted from "marketing site with CMS" to "research publication and multi-event hub." Adds Drizzle + Neon Postgres for typed application data, deployed on Cloudflare Pages for global edge delivery. 564 commits, 534 solo (94.7%).

Shanghai Summit 2026 (June 22–25) drew 20 confirmed speakers headlined by Ida Tin — the Clue co-founder who coined the term "FemTech" in 2016. Multi-tier sponsorship; AVPN among named partners. Every page rendered through infrastructure I designed and operate solo.

Two original research reports published through the platform: FemTech Market Map for Greater China (May 2025), FemmeHealth Ventures Alliance announcement (June 2025).

positions.title: Open Source Contributor
positions.description:
Two merged PRs in CopilotKit (36,100+ GitHub stars) — the open-source framework for building in-app AI copilots and agents — both publicly approved by named maintainers. Both contributions came directly from production systems I run.

PR #2068 — FemTracker Agent demo (merged September 2025, after 10 weeks of review). Contributed an 8-agent women's-health companion to CopilotKit's official demos_2025 collection: a Main Coordinator routes requests to specialists in cycle tracking, fertility, symptoms, nutrition, exercise, lifestyle, and health insights, using WHO-standard scoring scales.

Maintainer Tyler Slaton: "This is awesome! Thank you for the cool showcase @ChanMeng666."

PR #2529 — Claude Code MCP setup guide (merged October 2025). Expanded CopilotKit's MCP (Model Context Protocol — Anthropic's open standard for AI agents to safely use tools and data) documentation with step-by-step Claude Code setup — bridging CopilotKit's React framework with Anthropic's Claude Code CLI.

Maintainer Brandon McConnell: "This looks great. Thanks, @ChanMeng666 👏"

Stack of the FemTracker demo: Next.js 15 + React 19 + TypeScript on the frontend; Python 3.12 + LangGraph for multi-agent orchestration; Supabase Postgres; Redis caching at 90%+ hit rate.

positions.title: Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
positions.description:
CTO of Sanicle.Cloud — a US-based FemTech B2B SaaS tackling menstrual and menopause workplace wellness. Direct managers: CEO Paige Afanu (Omopeju Afanu) and Chief Period Officer Chaste Christopher Inegbedion. Peers: CXO Gabrielle J. Hurst, Chief AI Officer Daryll Hall.

Headline business outcome: IBM Silver Partner certification for Sanicle. My integration of IBM watsonx AI (running Llama 3.1 405B) inside employee dashboards was the engineering work that anchored Sanicle's entry into the IBM Business Partner Network. Sanicle.Cloud is now listed in IBM's official PartnerPlus Solution Directory. The same partnership positioned Sanicle for strategic conversations with Google for investment.

What I owned (5 production codebases, ~350+ commits, ~98% solo):

• Sanicle.Cloud corporate site (migrated off legacy Bubble no-code after I recommended it).
• Sanicle.AI — multi-tenant FemTech platform with role-separated dashboards (Employee / HR Manager / Org Admin), cycle tracking, AI consultations, leave-request workflows, HR analytics.
• Dual-AI resilience: IBM watsonx primary, Google Gemini fallback when watsonx exceeds Vercel's timeout ceiling.
• Mobile PWA prototype + executive demo deck used in IBM partner conversations.

Public Next.js contribution: published a multi-cause production fix for a NextAuth 5 + Next.js 15 auth-redirect failure on the Vercel community forum. Publicly thanked by Vercel Staff (Amy Egan). Featured in THISDAYLIVE (May 2025) on FemTech founders.

positions.title: Senior AI/ML Infrastructure Engineer
positions.description:
Hired as Senior AI/ML Infrastructure Engineer in February 2025. The role covered Sanicle's intense active-build phase — 14 weeks of solo development that took the company from a legacy Bubble no-code site to a production B2B SaaS platform.

Two flagship products designed and shipped:

• Sanicle.Cloud corporate website (Next.js 15 + React 19) — replaced the legacy no-code site after I recommended the migration. Integrated IBM watsonx AI as a conversational customer-service assistant driving visitor engagement.
• Sanicle.AI — core multi-tenant FemTech SaaS with role-separated dashboards for employees and HR. Includes predictive absenteeism alerts (so HR can plan around health-driven leave patterns) and HRIS integration.

Engineering achievements that translated directly to business outcomes:

• Integrated IBM Cloud watsonx AI inside employee dashboards — AI health consultations and automated leave requests. Anchored Sanicle's IBM Silver Partner certification.
• Built secure multi-tenant architecture (one platform serving multiple employer organisations safely) with end-to-end encryption and GDPR compliance — PostgreSQL + Drizzle, NextAuth 5 with bcrypt + OAuth, Employee / HR Manager / Org Admin roles.
• Designed Vercel + Upstash Redis infrastructure for enterprise-scale tenants.

This is the work that carried Sanicle into formal partnership conversations with IBM and Google.
media: Sanicle.cloud is an AI-powered SaaS platform helping employers scale menstrual and menopause wellness benefits for healthier workplaces.

positions.title: Software Developer
positions.location: Auckland Central, Auckland, New Zealand · Remote
positions.description:
Joined Crimson Academies' newly-formed Bandicoot engineering squad as Software Engineer for Aotearoa Infinite Academy (AIA) — New Zealand's first free online charter high school, confirmed October 14, 2025, opening Term 1 2026. Reports directly to founder/CEO Penelope Barton.

Four parallel tracks:

• ESOL learning platform — designed and shipped solo, end-to-end. Blank slate to v2 deployment in the first weekend. 6-role dashboard (System Admin / School Admin / Department Head / Teacher / Student / Parent), conversation transcription, audio recording for AI dialogue practice. UI/UX iterated against feedback from CGA Director of Curriculum & Pedagogy Becky Hancock.
• LMS monorepo refactor (Emi → Infinite, multi-tenant). Took the existing Firestore-based Emi LMS and refactored it onto Firebase DataConnect + PostgreSQL (Cloud SQL) — one Firebase project safely hosting two distinct school deployments (Emi Primary + AIA's Infinite-Learn) with shared auth and a single source-of-truth database.
• Led Bandicoot's first systematic AI Tools Training Session (Wispr Flow, Claude Skills, Claude Code, Specification-Driven Development via Spec Kit, Mermaid).
• SMS/email vendor analysis (Twilio + Resend) with pricing breakdown for the Emi project.

Open-source artefact launched here: claude-code-audio-hooks — explicitly preserved as my IP in the subsequent Engram Architect Cohort engagement letter.

positions.title: Mentor - Technology & Professional Development
positions.description:
Technology & Professional Development Mentor for one full annual cycle inside Forward with Her (她行 / taxing-mentorship) — a women-in-STEM mentor-mentee network connecting 360+ mentors with 800+ mentees globally across software engineering, data, product, and AI.

Independently-attested impact: seven of the twenty-three public LinkedIn recommendations on my profile come from mentees who specifically credit Forward with Her sessions — the single-largest source of attested mentorship impact across my career.

What I delivered: 1:1 mentorship across software engineering, AI tooling, and career navigation; group Office Hours; public lectures on AI programming, identity formation in tech, and career navigation for early-career women; a bilingual EN/CN promo site for the programme (3D Next.js 15 + Three.js, Cloudflare Pages).

The teaching doctrine I built here — "natural language is the source code"; ship a real production site by Week 8 — later became the foundation for the 2026 TechNest AI Track curriculum.

positions.title: Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
positions.description:
Solo CTO at FreePeriod — a Guangzhou-based public-health startup tackling period poverty through emergency sanitary-pad dispensers, reproductive-health education, and policy advocacy. Founded 2024 by Julia Zhu, incubated at HKUST(Guangzhou) Lab of Future Technology. The product combines software with off-grid solar-powered hardware deployed in public spaces.

Joined during the company's external-validation cycle. Six named awards in 2024–2025, with the website I built as the public artefact every judging panel was clicking through:

• Best Award — Japan's 12th International Conference on Social Sciences and Humanities
• Third Prize — HKUST Sustainable Smart Living Competition
• Best Social Media — China's inaugural FemTech Weekend Challenge
• First Prize — Shenzhen Nanshan District Philanthropic Creative Star Contest
• Selected — Tencent Technology Venture Capital Program
• Selected — Tsinghua University SDG Open Innovation Marathon
• HKUST Entrepreneurship Bridge Grant

Built and shipped the official site end-to-end, solo — 53 commits, 100% authored by me.

What the site does:

• Interactive Google Maps directory of dispenser locations — visitors find free menstrual products near them; each rollout is one git commit with full audit history.
• Fully bilingual (EN / CN) — serving domestic Chinese policy partners and international academic/grant audiences.
• Document and video preview pipelines for advocacy briefs and partnership decks.

Stayed on post-tenure as long-term technical custodian; executed Vercel → Cloudflare Pages migration in April 2026 without rebuilding the app.

positions.title: Backend Developer
positions.description:
Selected into ByteDance's Youth Training Camp — in the Team 115. ByteDance is the parent of Douyin (China's TikTok), TikTok, Toutiao, CapCut, Lark, and Doubao. One of the most competitive training entry points into China's consumer-internet stack.

Delivered a deliberately overcomplete portfolio (most trainees deliver the algorithm submissions and team capstone; I added three artefacts beyond scope):

• Algorithm curriculum — solved every problem in ByteDance MarsCode's Juejin track. 81 problems, fully written up with statement, approach, complexity analysis, and tested Python implementation (juejin-algorithm-practice, 66 commits, 100% solo).
• Team 115 capstone — Spring Boot 3 e-commerce platform (Douyin Mall): user management, products, cart, orders, AI customer service. #2-most-prolific committer on an 8-person team (45 of 174 commits, ~26%), behind only the team lead. Stack: Java 17 + Spring Boot 3 + MyBatis + MySQL + Redis + Alibaba Cloud + Docker.
• Solo Vue 3 frontend supplement (beyond scope) — built to integrate against the team backend, Cypress + Vitest coverage.
• Two open-source teaching templates (beyond scope), both bilingual EN + CN: Go + Gin (douyin-mall-go-template — 52 stars / 7 forks, my most-starred OSS project) and Spring Boot + Maven. Both now used by later camp cohorts as scaffolding.
media: Front-end, Back-end, Big data -- MarsCode Youth Camp

positions.title: Full Stack Developer
positions.location: Christchurch City, Canterbury, New Zealand · Hybrid
positions.description:
CORDE — a 30+-year-old, 164-staff Canterbury infrastructure and civil-construction company (CCNZ Canterbury Contractor of the Year; Construction Excellence National Award). Five months on-site at CORDE's Rolleston head office, reporting to Barry Clark (business sponsor) with Kullum Ladley as Workbench API specification author.

The business problem: CORDE's field crews work in rural Canterbury where mobile coverage drops out, but the company's central operations platform assumes connectivity. They needed a mobile app that could become the system of record for the moment of work and reconcile cleanly when connectivity returned.

What I built and shipped:

• Production-quality React Native offline-first mobile app — the field worker's tablet captures text, photos, and GPS coordinates simultaneously, stores them locally with integrity guarantees, and syncs back to Workbench on reconnection. Handles real conflict cases: duplicate IDs, partial sync, mid-batch connectivity drops.
• #1-most-prolific contributor — 227 of 539 commits (42%).
• Effectively sole author of the team documentation repository (140 of 143 commits, 97.9%).
• The app remains in active use today by CORDE's field crews — kept current with React Native upgrades and Android 16 KB page-size compliance.

Stack: React Native 0.83 + TypeScript + Native Base + SQLite + native geolocation/camera/maps.

education.description:
Master of Applied Computing with Distinction (top grade band) and Dean's List (top 5% of cohort) from Lincoln University, NZ. The programme bridged my geography/GIS foundation into modern software engineering — the same pivot I now make in every AI and cultural-tech project I ship.

What the programme covered:

• Studio model — enterprise software shipped to real industry clients using object-oriented design and agile workflows. The practice that prepared me to run solo CTO and Founding Engineer work.
• Advanced database systems — transaction processing, security, NoSQL, schema design and optimisation. The foundation behind every multi-tenant Postgres + Drizzle system I've shipped since.
• Applied neural networks — model calibration, data visualisation, real-world problem framing.
• Geographic Information Systems (GIS) + IT specialisation, building directly on my Geography Science Bachelor's.

NZQA-verifiable credential ID: 170086552.

education.activitiesAndSocieties: Field Practice in Geology · Regional Geography internships · spatial-data analysis projects on university-grade GIS facilities.
education.description:
Bachelor of Geography Science with Distinction from Jiangsu Normal University, China. Specialised in Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and Remote Sensing — the spatial-data foundation that later carried me into software engineering, AI, and the GIS + IT specialisation of my Lincoln Master's.

Core coursework:

• GIS + Remote Sensing — spatial data analysis, cartography, working with university-grade GIS facilities and faculty.
• Computer Applications and Programming (Visual Basic) — top scores of 96 and 86 on practical software-build exams; my first formal programming credentials.
• Advanced Mathematics, Economic Geography, Urban Planning — first-principles reasoning across environmental, economic, and built-environment systems.

Why this matters for an AI engineer: spatial reasoning and bridging numerical models with real-world systems are the same muscles I apply to multi-agent AI architecture today.

education.activitiesAndSocieties: Student Council — leadership roles, inter-class academic competitions, and cross-disciplinary science-track collaborative projects across multiple years.
education.description:
Founded in 1906, Nanning No.2 High School is one of Guangxi's flagship secondary schools and ranks among China's top 100 — a rigorous environment with nationally-recognised teachers and a long science-track tradition.

I studied the science stream (Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology) and graduated A+. This is where the first-principles analytical mindset that later anchored my Geographic Information Systems work, my software engineering, and now my AI-agent architecture practice was first formed.

Active in Student Council across multiple years — leadership, inter-class competitions, and collaborative projects that sharpened the working habits I still rely on every day as a solo CTO and Founding Engineer.

projects.name: Eatropolis — Auckland Culinary Festival Platform (Chow Luck Club × Tātaki Auckland Unlimited)
projects.description:
The official event website for Eatropolis — Auckland's award-winning culinary festival, at Shed 10 on Queens Wharf on 10 October 2026. Visitors buy tickets, browse the chef lineup, read the programme, and can ask a streaming AI host on the page for guidance instead of digging through nav.

Solo-built freelance commission under a signed 4 May 2026 agreement with Chow Luck Club Ltd (Director Bee Keng Koh; PM Armand Bentigan). Event partner: Tātaki Auckland Unlimited (Auckland Council).

• Engineered for 1,000 concurrent visitors under k6 load testing per contract.
• Hardened to NZ Government Web Accessibility Standard 1.2 (WCAG 2.2 AA) with Tātaki Auckland Unlimited review gates before each launch phase.
• Streaming OpenAI chat + Whisper + TTS gated behind Cloudflare Turnstile and per-IP KV rate-limiting.
• GitHub-as-CMS so the non-technical client edits MDX directly in-browser via one-click web-editor links.
• 115 solo commits in 9 days.

Stack: Next.js 16, OpenNext-on-Cloudflare-Workers, Drizzle + Neon Postgres, Resend, OpenAI streaming.

projects.name: Seismophone — Trilingual Suno Music-Lineage Explorer (EN / CN / JP)
projects.description:
Seismophone is an independent observatory for AI music that maps how a Suno-generated song's lineage descends from its parent. Suno is the AI music platform; its own product doesn't expose lineage to creators. Seismophone does, because the research behind it discovered that Suno's API serializer leaks the parent→child UUID chain only on the trending feed and persona sub-trees — nowhere else, not even on the remix page.

• Three open public lenses (creator profiles, trending, lineage view) + single-purchase deep-dive reports, each a deterministic analysis of one creator's own catalog.
• Lineage traversal uses WITH RECURSIVE CTEs in Postgres so the graph walk happens at the database, not the application layer.
• Trilingual EN / CN / JP with cookie-driven server-resolved locale.
• oEmbed-discovered compliance posture; no scraping, no headless browsers.
• 46 solo commits across one month — same-day scaffold-to-live, then production hardening: Stripe webhook verification and a credits ledger that auto-switches keys by STRIPE_SECRET_KEY prefix.

Stack: Next.js 16, Drizzle + Neon Postgres, Stripe, Resend, Docker + Traefik VPS.

projects.name: Vitex — AI Career Agent SaaS (vitex.org.nz)
projects.description:
Vitex turns a job description plus a candidate's background into an ATS-optimised resume PDF and matching cover letter in about 30 seconds. The user pastes a job ad, uploads their existing CV, and an 8-step AI pipeline streams the tailored documents back. A natural-language refinement box then lets them iterate — "make the leadership section stronger" — and the AI regenerates just that slice.

• Pipeline: JD parsing → background parsing → match analysis → resume tailoring → ATS scoring → cover letter → template render (Typst source) → PDF compile, streamed via SSE.
• Typst compiles each PDF locally in under 100ms — the user sees the document before finishing the reasoning summary.
• Three production migrations (including a LaTeX → Typst engine swap) shipped with zero outage.
• Stripe credit ledger; public shareable links via per-document tokens for organic distribution.

Stack: Next.js 15, Vercel AI SDK + gpt-5.5 (reasoning) / gpt-5.4-mini (extract), Typst, Stripe, Neon Postgres + Drizzle, Docker + Traefik on DigitalOcean.

projects.name: ArchLang — A Programming Language I Invented That Compiles to Professional Floor Plans
projects.description:
ArchLang is a programming language I designed for architecture: you write a floor plan as code — walls, rooms, doors, windows, fixtures, dimensions — and it compiles to deterministic, professional SVG (also DXF, PDF, PNG, text). Think Typst or LaTeX, but for building plans instead of documents.

• Hand-written lexer + recursive-descent parser in pure TypeScript with zero runtime dependencies — the same compiler runs in Node and in the browser.
• Exports structured diagnostics instead of throwing, with lint / describe / repair pipelines for architectural-soundness checking and circulation analysis.
• Ships a CLI with JSON output built for AI agents, plus VS Code / LSP tooling and a live browser playground.
• Published to npm as @chanmeng666/archlang with its own documentation site.

Stack: TypeScript, Node.js, Vite, CodeMirror 6, LSP, Vitest.

projects.name: ArchCanvas — AI Design Agent for Architects That Productizes ArchLang
projects.description:
ArchCanvas turns my ArchLang language into a product for architects. Describe a brief (or drop in reference images) and it generates real SVG floor plans through the ArchLang compiler plus gpt-5.5 orchestration + GPT Image 2 renderings grounded on the compiled plan, then lets you iterate on an infinite zoomable canvas with an AI review mode that leaves anchored findings you can fix in one click.

• Sister product to my ArchLang language — consumes @chanmeng666/archlang as a package, so it produces editable parametric plans, not just images.
• AI review mode surfaces anchored findings directly on the canvas with one-click "have AI fix".
• Export any project as a real git repo (isomorphic-git + memfs).
• Refinement mutates the plan in place — edits, parametric tweaks and re-renders never regenerate it from scratch.

Stack: Next.js, React 19, Vercel AI SDK + OpenAI (gpt-5.5 + GPT Image 2; GPT-4o for vision), @chanmeng666/archlang, Neon Postgres + Drizzle, Stripe, Cloudinary.

projects.name: echook — Audio Notification Hooks for Claude Code / Cursor / Codex CLI
projects.description:
echook adds audio cues to AI coding assistants so you stop staring at the terminal waiting for them to finish. Thirty-seven hook events — task started, permission needed, build failed, agent stopped, rate-limit hit, context nearing 100% — each play a sound and optionally fire Slack / Discord / Teams / ntfy webhooks. Same product on Claude Code, Cursor IDE, and Codex CLI. Install via natural language ("install echook with the Focus Flow theme").

• 42 releases, 248 tests on triple-platform CI (Ubuntu / Windows / macOS × Python 3.9 / 3.12 / 3.13). 37 hook events, 2 themes.
• Context-window status line with live quota bars — see remaining budget without alt-tabbing.
• Explicitly preserved as the author's IP in the Engram Architect Cohort Engagement Letter (9 May 2026); Engram is in Anthropic's Partner Network.
• Sister repo echook-promo-video: 88.7-second product film built in React + Remotion + AI-generated audio — zero Premiere, zero voice actors.

Stack: Python 3.6+, Claude Code Plugin SDK, ElevenLabs TTS, webhooks, GitHub Actions CI.

projects.name: She Sharp Member Platform — AI Mentor-Matching SaaS for NZ's Largest Women-in-Tech Community
projects.description:
The member-lifecycle platform that now runs She Sharp — NZ's largest women-in-tech community (charitable trust CC57025; 2,200+ members, 1,000+ active, 50+ corporate partners, 84+ events since 2014). Members get matched to mentors by an AI scoring engine with admin approval, browse events, pay through Stripe; the volunteer team gets automated Slack digests instead of doing it by hand.

• AI mentor–mentee matcher: GPT-4o-mini scoring pairs across five weighted dimensions (Skill 35%, Goal 30%, MBTI 15%, Industry 10%, Logistics 10%) with explainable reasons and a rule-based fallback; admin as final gate.
• 793 of 936 commits solo (85%) across ~12 months. Frontend designer Lesley Gao authored 140 (15%).
• Slack ecosystem: weekly stats digest, NZ funding-opportunities crawler over six government sources, Claude Code skill that publishes new events with one dry-run-default command.

Stack: Next.js 15, Drizzle + Neon Postgres, NextAuth 5, Stripe, Slack, Vercel.

projects.name: FemTech Weekend Gen-2 Platform — Docusaurus + Drizzle on Cloudflare Pages
projects.description:
The official site and submissions hub for FemTech Weekend — China's first women's-health-technology organisation — which delivered Shanghai Summit 2026 (June 22–25). 20 confirmed speakers were headlined by Ida Tin, the Clue co-founder who coined the term "FemTech" in 2016.

Gen-2 was a deliberate rewrite from Next.js to Docusaurus when the site's centre of gravity shifted from "marketing site" to "research publication and multi-event hub". Visitors today read original research reports (FemTech Market Map for Greater China; FemmeHealth Ventures Alliance), browse the speaker programme, and submit pitches / programme entries / speaker applications / ecosystem partnerships through four review pipelines.

• 534 of 564 commits solo (94.7%) over ~13.5 months.
• Bilingual EN / CN from day one — the non-technical founder Zhu Yihan edits the live site directly.
• 15 Cloudflare Pages Functions (~3,839 LOC) replace a separate backend; submissions + email + admin review run on edge serverless.

Stack: Docusaurus 3.9, React 18, Drizzle + Neon Postgres, Cloudflare Pages, Resend, Notion-as-CMS.

projects.name: programming.chanmeng.org — 5-Cohort Bilingual AI Programming Teaching Platform with RAG Assistant
projects.description:
The bilingual teaching site behind every AI programming cohort I've run since late 2024 — Forward with Her (2024-winter, 2025-summer), Her Waka 2026 (NZ Ministry of Social Development funded, at academyEX Auckland), Peyvand Academy 2026, and TechNest 2026 (Canada). All five cohorts coexist on one platform; each student gets their own version-aware course.

• In-course AI assistant: ~4,800 bilingual document chunks indexed with RAG (retrieval-augmented generation — the AI looks up the cohort's actual materials before answering, so students don't get hallucinations from the wrong version).
• 211 of 220 non-merge commits solo (~96%); the remainder are bot commits (Claude code-review automation + Dependabot).
• Notion-as-CMS classroom: course-wide message board, capstone-showcase page with live audience voting, Typst PDF handouts auto-built from MDX.
• Bilingual EN / CN with Algolia contextual search.

Stack: Docusaurus 3.8, Cloudflare Pages, Llama 3.1 8B via Cloudflare Workers AI + Vectorize, Notion API, Algolia, Typst.

projects.name: Tam-AI-Ti — Voice-First AI Financial-Wellness Coach (Te Whare Tapa Whā)
projects.description:
Tam-AI-Ti is a voice-first AI financial-wellness coach grounded in Te Whare Tapa Whā — the Māori model of holistic health where physical, mental, spiritual, and family wellbeing are inseparable from financial decisions. Users talk to the coach in te reo Māori or English; the coach reflects back in the same cultural frame, not as a Western budgeting app with a cultural wrapper.

Commissioned by Riria (Missy) Te Kanawa — former KPMG NZ Māori Sector Partner, now ASB Bank Māori Executive Lead — introduced by Dr Mahsa Mohaghegh.

• 351 solo commits over six months. 48-table / 494-column schema across 10 modules where every Hauora dimension is a typed column, not UI decoration: culture is in the schema.
• 19-user research cohort (Oct 2025 – Mar 2026): 181 bilingual journal entries, 35 voice sessions, 146 AI coach messages — sustained engagement on a pre-commercial product with zero marketing.
• Voice via GPT-4o-realtime with server-side VAD.

Stack: Next.js 15.5, React 19.1, Drizzle + Neon Postgres + pgVector (1536-dim), OpenAI (gpt-4o-mini / gpt-4o / gpt-4o-realtime), CopilotKit.

projects.name: Sanicle.AI — Multi-Tenant FemTech SaaS with IBM watsonx
projects.description:
Sanicle.AI is the multi-tenant FemTech platform that lets US employers offer menstrual and menopause workplace-wellness support without forcing employees to expose anything personal to HR. Employees get a private dashboard for cycle tracking, AI consultations, and leave requests; HR Managers see only de-identified, aggregate analytics so they can plan around real health patterns instead of guesses.

• Integrated IBM watsonx (running Llama 3.1 405B) inside the employee dashboard — the engineering work that anchored Sanicle's IBM Silver Partner certification and PartnerPlus directory listing.
• Dual-AI resilience: watsonx primary, Google Gemini fallback when watsonx exceeds Vercel's function-timeout ceiling — no user sees a stalled assistant.
• ~98% solo across ~350 commits over 12 months. A NextAuth 5 auth-redirect production fix I published was acknowledged by Vercel Staff Amy Egan on the Vercel community forum.

Stack: Next.js 15, React 19, NextAuth 5, Drizzle + Neon Postgres, Upstash Redis, Vercel AI SDK, IBM watsonx + Google Gemini fallback.
media: Sanicle-AI: Women's Health Platform for Workplace Wellness

projects.name: Sanicle.Cloud — Corporate Site with IBM watsonx Conversational Assistant
projects.description:
Sanicle.Cloud is the public-facing website that introduces Sanicle's workplace-wellness products to employers, HR brokers, and journalists covering FemTech. Visitors can click through the standard sections (platform, solutions, team, ROI calculator, demo request) or just ask an embedded IBM watsonx-powered assistant in natural language and skip the navigation entirely.

• Recommended and executed the migration off Sanicle's legacy Bubble no-code site — the case was that no-code couldn't support enterprise authentication, multi-tenant data, watsonx integration, or Vercel-native CI/CD. The team accepted; the migration shipped solo.
• Built on Next.js 15 with watsonx (Llama 3.1 405B) as the conversational layer. The watsonx integration carried Sanicle into IBM's Business Partner Network and the PartnerPlus directory listing.

Stack: Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind, IBM watsonx, Vercel.

projects.name: Google News MCP Server — Early-Ecosystem AI Tool-Use Server
projects.description:
A Model Context Protocol (MCP — Anthropic's open standard that lets AI agents safely use real tools and data) server that gives AI assistants live access to Google News with topical categorisation, region/language targeting, and full-coverage drill-down. Used by people whose AI assistant needs to know what happened today, not what was true at the training cutoff.

• Shipped Dec 30, 2024 — 35 days after Anthropic's Nov 25, 2024 MCP launch. One of the earliest community MCP servers in existence.
• Carries the PulseMCP "Top Pick" badge with 26.1k visitors and a #1,105 global popularity rank; Glama gives it an A license rating.
• Listed across 15+ MCP catalogs (Smithery, mcp.so, Glama, PulseMCP, Awesome MCP Servers, MCP Server Finder, mcpservers.org, AgentSkillsHub, and more) and featured in three independent deep-dives, including Skywork AI's "AI Engineer's Guide".

Stack: TypeScript + Node.js + @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + SerpAPI; published as @chanmeng666/google-news-server on npm.

projects.name: CORDE Mobile — Offline-First Field-Operations App (React Native)
projects.description:
A React Native field-operations app for CORDE — a 30+-year-old, 164-staff Canterbury civil-construction contractor whose crews work in rural areas where mobile coverage drops out. The tablet becomes the system of record at the moment of work: text + photos + GPS + timestamps captured locally, integrity-checked, and reconciled to CORDE's central Workbench platform on reconnection — including the awkward cases: duplicate IDs, partial sync, mid-batch drops.

• #1-most-prolific contributor on the codebase: 227 of 539 commits (42%), ahead of teammates Luke Shi (28.6%) and Xue Zheng (25.8%).
• Sole author of the team documentation repository (140 of 143 commits, 97.9%), built against 15 Workbench API specifications authored by Kullum Ladley.
• Still in active use today by CORDE's field crews; kept current through React Native upgrades and Android 16 KB page-size compliance.

Stack: React Native 0.83, TypeScript, Native Base, SQLite, native geolocation / camera / image-picker / file-system / secure keychain.
media: CORDE Mobile Application: Revolutionizing Field Operations

projects.name: FreePeriod — Bilingual Period-Poverty Platform (Software + Hardware)
projects.description:
The public site for FreePeriod — a Guangzhou-incubated startup tackling period poverty through solar-powered emergency sanitary-pad dispensers, reproductive-health education, and policy advocacy. Visitors land on a bilingual page, locate the nearest free-pad dispenser on an interactive Google Maps directory, and read FreePeriod's policy briefs and partnership decks.

The site was the public artefact every judging panel clicked through during FreePeriod's six-award streak in 2024–25:

• Best Award, Japan 12th Int'l Conference on Social Sciences & Humanities
• Third Prize, HKUST Sustainable Smart Living Competition
• Best Social Media, China's inaugural FemTech Weekend Challenge
• First Prize, Shenzhen Nanshan Philanthropic Creative Star Contest
• Selected, Tencent Technology Venture Capital Program
• Selected, Tsinghua University SDG Open Innovation Marathon

53 commits, 100% solo. Stayed on after CTO tenure as long-term custodian and executed the Vercel → Cloudflare Pages migration in April 2026.

Stack: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind, Google Maps API, Cloudflare Pages.

volunteering.organization: Forward with Her 她行 Mentorship
volunteering.description:
Volunteer STEM Mentor at Forward with Her (她行) — a women-in-STEM nonprofit founded in 2020 connecting 1,000+ members worldwide through one-on-one and group mentorship, with the "Her Voice" podcast amplifying women's voices in technology.

What I do as a volunteer mentor:

• Bi-weekly 1:1 meetings with assigned early-career women in technology, covering career development, personal growth, and practical problem-solving in STEM environments.
• Group mentoring sessions and shared Office Hours alongside cohort sisters across software engineering, data, product, and AI.
• Coordinated through a dedicated volunteer liaison so mentee assignments, communication, and certification stay organised end-to-end.
• Holds 2024 and 2025 official Forward with Her mentor certificates.

Independently-attested impact: seven of the twenty-three public LinkedIn recommendations on my profile come from mentees who specifically credit Forward with Her sessions for their learning — making this the single-largest source of attested mentorship outcomes across my career.

The teaching doctrine I developed here — "natural language is the source code"; ship a real production site by Week 8; screenshot-as-evidence assessment — later became the foundation for the 2026 TechNest Community AI Track curriculum I now teach as the sole AI Instructor.
media: My Acceptance Email from Forward with Her (她行) as an Official Mentor

volunteering.description:
Volunteer translator and cultural-exchange facilitator for a Māori dance troupe from New Zealand performing at the 2023 Guilin Arts Festival (Guangxi, China). Provided live translation support across traditional dance, music, and language — facilitating deeper cultural exchange between the performers and local audiences.

What the three-month placement covered:

• Live translation between the visiting Māori troupe and Guilin Festival organisers, audiences, and partner organisations.
• Direct engagement with Māori cultural practice — traditional dance, music, te reo Māori language, and ceremonial protocol — through immersive exposure rather than textbook study.
• Cross-cultural communication and cultural representation in international settings, using IT tools to enrich how Māori culture was presented to diverse Chinese audiences.

Why this matters to my current work: the immersive exposure to Māori culture I gained at Guilin in 2023 became the experiential foundation that later anchored Tam-AI-Ti — the AI Financial Wellness Coach I built solo on the Te Whare Tapa Whā holistic-health framework, as an independent research commission from Riria "Missy" Te Kanawa personally (former KPMG NZ National Māori Sector lead; now Māori Executive Lead at ASB Bank), to explore Māori financial prosperity. What started as festival volunteer work two years earlier became the cultural grounding behind one of my flagship AI projects.

Volunteer at the Maori Song and Dance Troupe performance

volunteering.description:
Volunteer Educator with Cebu International Academy (CIA) — the Philippines-based language and professional-skills academy where I completed IELTS and Business English programmes in focused small-group training as an alumna.

While in Cebu, I led community outreach programmes in rural areas across multiple villages, organising events and distributing essential supplies to children, women, and the elderly — extending CIA's reach beyond classroom walls into the surrounding communities where the academy operates.

What the six-month engagement reinforced:

• Business communication skills, sharpened daily by English-immersion teaching, small-group facilitation, and direct cross-cultural exchange with learners across multiple Filipino communities.
• Event organisation muscle — coordinating volunteer teams, supply logistics, and community-engagement schedules across rural sites.
• A commitment to driving positive social impact through technology. This is the orientation that later anchored my work in women's-health tech (CTO at Sanicle, FemTech Weekend, and FreePeriod) and in culturally-grounded AI (Tam-AI-Ti — my Māori financial-wellness coach, built as an independent research commission).

honorsAndAwards.title: Outstanding Mentor Award - AI Hackathon Festival 2025
honorsAndAwards.issuer: AI Forum NZ, She Sharp, and Auckland University of Technology
media: Technical guidance to Westlake Girls High School students
honorsAndAwards.description:
Named an Outstanding Mentor at the AI Hackathon Festival 2025 — a two-day event co-organised by AI Forum NZ, She Sharp, and Auckland University of Technology that challenged teams to build AI solutions aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

Served as one of 14 expert mentors guiding 11 teams and 80+ participants through the full build, from idea to working prototype inside a 24-hour window. The participants ranged from Westlake Girls High School students to working professionals across multiple industries.

What the mentoring covered:
• Helping high-school students use AI tools effectively and understand where they genuinely help
• Guiding adult competitors in turning a rough concept into a functional Minimum Viable Product under time pressure
• Hands-on support across AI/ML implementation, full-stack engineering, and system architecture
• Ethical-AI considerations and user-centred design, so the solutions were responsible as well as working

Worked alongside teams tackling real problems — food insecurity, digital accessibility, workforce upskilling, and indigenous environmental stewardship — giving each the technical depth to ship something demonstrable by the deadline.

media: Commission on the Status of Women

honorsAndAwards.description:

Presented to an audience of international delegates, UN officials, and global leaders, demonstrating how AI can address workplace gender inequities through practical, privacy-respecting health solutions rather than policy alone.

The presentation drew direct response from senior figures:
• IBM representatives expressed interest in a pilot collaboration
• The Minister of Gender and Children's Affairs from Sierra Leone praised the platform as the first comprehensive solution connecting menstrual-cycle data to actionable workplace policy through AI analysis

Speaking at a UN forum of this stature placed an independently built FemTech platform in front of the people who shape global gender-equity policy — and turned a technical demo into concrete partnership interest.

received.headline: Founder & CEO at GAVIGO | Activation and Execution Platform for Interactive Software
received.text:
Chan has been one of the most important engineering contributors to GAVIGO during a demanding early stage.

What stands out about Chan is not only her technical ability, but the way she turns ambiguous founder-level direction into working systems, measurable proof, and reliable product surfaces.

At GAVIGO, Chan has led critical work across our activation and execution pipeline, orchestration behavior, telemetry, demo reliability, public/investor/operator surfaces, and compute-backed proof systems. She consistently brings clarity to hard systems questions: what needs to run at activation time, what can be prepared, what can be reused, where state can be preserved, and where the boundary needs to be measured rather than assumed.

She combines speed with discipline. Chan ships quickly, but she also documents, tests, and pushes for technical honesty. If something is unsafe to expose, she says it clearly. If a fast fix is enough, she executes it. If a deeper system boundary appears, she helps turn it into a measured engineering question instead of hand-waving around it.

She has also been an excellent founder-engineer collaborator. In an early-stage company, priorities change, constraints are real, and the path is rarely linear. Chan has handled that environment with ownership, patience, and maturity. She cares not only about writing code, but about making the system demonstrable, explainable, and credible for investors, partners, and the team.

Chan played a major role in helping GAVIGO move from an early technical concept into a controlled proof system with real product behavior, measured infrastructure signals, and a much clearer activation/execution architecture.

I would strongly recommend Chan for any ambitious technical team building low-latency systems, activation infrastructure, orchestration, AI/compute-backed product surfaces, or early-stage deep technology. She is the kind of engineer who makes a hard system more real, more measurable, and more shippable.

publications.title: [Weekly AI Digest] What's Shipping in AI
publications.author: Chan Meng (author)
publications.description:
A weekly newsletter I write — every Monday, curated picks from the hottest new AI tools on Product Hunt, condensed into a three-minute read that keeps subscribers current on what's trending in AI.

Free, with a five-second subscribe. It's the lightweight, tools-focused counterpart to the deeper research breakdowns in Decoding the Future.

publications.title: Meet the FemTech Founders Redefining Women's Health at Work
publications.author: THISDAYLIVE editorial — Chan Meng featured
publications.description:
A press feature profiling the founding team behind Sanicle and its mission to redefine women's health in the workplace, published during my tenure as CTO at Sanicle.

The article situates Sanicle's work within the wider FemTech movement — building technology that treats women's workplace health as core infrastructure rather than an afterthought — and features the people driving it. Being profiled here put the platform's engineering and product vision in front of an international readership focused on health innovation.

publications.title: [Podcast — host] Decoding the Future
publications.author: Chan Meng (host)
publications.description:
An English-language podcast I host, exploring the landmark research papers driving innovation in Artificial Intelligence, Large Language Models, and beyond — translating dense research into something a working engineer or curious listener can actually use.

The show runs alongside a companion LinkedIn newsletter of the same name, with 800+ subscribers, so each episode reaches both audio and reading audiences.

publications.title: [Podcast — host] Future Turing 未来图灵
publications.author: Chan Meng (host)
publications.description:
The Chinese-language counterpart to Decoding the Future — a podcast I host that decodes landmark research papers in Artificial Intelligence, LLMs, and other cutting-edge fields for a Chinese audience.

The framing question of the show: "Where is the boundary of intelligence? How will the future be shaped by code?" Running parallel English and Chinese editions lets the same research reach listeners across both language communities.

publications.title: [Podcast] From Math Teacher to FemTech CTO: Chan Meng on AI and Women's Health Innovation
publications.author: Femtech At Work (host) — Chan Meng (guest)
publications.description:
Greater China Edition, Episode 21 of the Femtech At Work podcast — a conversation about my path into FemTech and how AI is being used to understand and improve women's health.

What the episode covers:
• My journey from teaching mathematics to becoming a CTO in tech — proof you can follow a non-linear path into the field
• Where the real innovations are happening in women's health technology
• How AI is being applied to women's health, and the considerations that come with it — data privacy chief among them
• The specific challenges of discussing and addressing women's health in a Chinese cultural context
• Down-to-earth advice for anyone thinking about starting their own venture in the women's-health space

publications.title: AI-Driven Health Solutions for Women in the Workplace — UN CSW69 & Columbia University SIPA
publications.author: FemTech Weekend editorial — Chan Meng featured
publications.description:
A feature in FemTech Weekend's publication on my work as an independent developer presenting at the UN Commission on the Status of Women (CSW69) and Columbia University SIPA's international forum on technology and women's health.

I presented "Sani," an AI health assistant that delivers personalised health recommendations for women in the workplace. The solution pairs a privacy-first architecture — protecting employee data while still surfacing actionable insight — with a dual-view dashboard that serves both employees and HR teams.

The presentation drew direct response from senior figures:
• IBM representatives expressed interest in a pilot collaboration
• The Minister of Gender and Children's Affairs from Sierra Leone praised it as the first comprehensive solution connecting menstrual-cycle data to actionable workplace policy through AI analysis

The feature reflects a consistent approach to FemTech: minimalist architecture and end-to-end full-stack delivery in service of women's health — "Less is More" applied to a hard problem.

publications.title: From Math Teacher to Full-Stack Developer
publications.publisher: 小宇宙FM — 码农姐妹 (Xiaoyuzhou FM, "Coder Sisters")
publications.author: 码农姐妹 (host) — Chan Meng (guest)
publications.description:
A guest appearance on 《码农姐妹》(Coder Sisters), a podcast from Xiaoyuzhou FM, where I shared my career transition from teaching maths to working as a full-stack developer.

We also talked about my minimalist lifestyle and my observations on the job markets in China, the US, and New Zealand, and I explained how I use AI to learn programming and how it accelerated my move into engineering — a practical look at retraining into tech rather than an abstract one.

publications.title: Weekly Pulse: Remote MCP, Images, Screen Recording
publications.author: Tadas Antanavicius (PulseMCP) — Chan Meng's MCP servers featured
publications.description:
Third-party coverage in the PulseMCP newsletter, where the Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers I built — server-google-news and server-google-jobs — were featured among the week's notable ecosystem contributions. MCP is Anthropic's open standard that lets AI agents safely use real tools and data.

Being picked up by an independent MCP-ecosystem newsletter, written by Tadas Antanavicius, is external validation of an early contribution to the agent-tooling space — the servers were useful enough that a curator chose to surface them to a specialist audience.
